text: Ocean City + Assateague Watersports — It's more than jet ski and boat rentals, it's an experience. Built by riders, for riders.

Jet skis that actually let you ride. Pontoon boats that go where others won't.

Ocean City & Assateague's Most Legendary Ride — 6 miles south to Assateague Island National Seashore.
This Isn't Your Average Jet Ski Rental.

While other Ocean City companies keep you circling the bay in a confined, buoyed-off zone — we take you on an adventure. From the inlet, you ride 6 miles down to the Verrazano Bridge along Assateague Island National Seashore — one of the longest open-water jet ski routes on the East Coast.
We built this route. Every other company talking about riding to Assateague is following our lead.
Wild horses. Dolphins. Hidden sandbars. Million-dollar views. This isn't a rental — it's a front-row seat to everything the bay has to offer.
They've roamed free since the 1600s. You'll see them right on the beach from your jet ski — no zoo, no fence, no tour bus. Just you and 400 years of wild.
Atlantic bottlenose dolphins breaking the surface right next to you. It happens so often we bring it up in every single pre-ride briefing.
Waterfront estates, private docks, mega yachts. OC from the water hits different — this is the side tourists never see.
6 miles of open water from the inlet to the Verrazano Bridge. No circles. No buoys. No one telling you to slow down. Pin it and ride.
The whole city glowing from the water — Ferris wheel, boardwalk, high-rises. The ride back is the photo everyone posts.
Reservations highly recommended — slots fill fast.
Follow the full southbound route to Assateague Island. Every stop, every landmark, every moment.
How fast do your jet skis go?

Our Sea-Doo GTX skis are capable of 40–42 mph on open water. Speed depends on conditions, weight, and currents. You have full throttle freedom — we're not running alongside you telling you to slow down.
Where do the jet skis ride?

Our jet ski route runs south from the inlet along Assateague Island down to the Verrazano Bridge — a 6-mile stretch of wide open water. You'll ride past wild horses, dolphins, hidden sandbars, and some of the most scenic coastline on the East Coast. This is a one-direction ride south, not a circle — the way jet skiing should be. We brief you on the full route before you launch.
Does instruction time count toward my ride time?

No. Your 70 minutes starts the moment you hit the water — not when you walk in the door, not when you sign waivers. That's why we ask you to arrive 45 minutes before your scheduled time. Show up early, get the full 70.
What is the minimum age to rent a jet ski in Ocean City?
You must be at least 16 years old to operate a ski. Riders under 21 require a parent or guardian present at the time of rental. All riders must sign a liability waiver.
How many people can ride on one jet ski?
You can absolutely ride with more than one person. Each ski holds 1–3 riders with a
Do I need experience to ride a jet ski?

None required. We give every rider a full orientation at the dock before you launch — controls, safety, boundaries, the route. If you can drive a car, you can ride a jet ski. Most first-timers are flying within 10 minutes.
What should I wear and bring?

Swimwear, sunscreen (apply before arriving — it washes off fast on the water), and a sense of adventure. We provide life jackets. Leave valuables, car keys, and phones in your vehicle or bring a waterproof case.
Are refunds available if I cancel?
All sales are final. No refunds given. In the rare event that we cancel due to extreme weather or mechanical issues on our end, you'll receive a full credit to rebook at your convenience.
Is parking available at your location?
Yes — there's parking available right at our location at 12817 Harbor Rd. See our
Book your jet ski today. Slots fill fast in peak season — and there's nothing like this anywhere else in Ocean City, MD.
Reserve Your Boat — Slots Fill Fast
Your private boat. Ocean City's entire bay. No strangers. No tour guide. Your group, your adventure.

This isn't a tour. There's no captain telling you where to go or how long to stay. You rent the boat — the whole thing, just for your group — and you captain your own adventure across Ocean City Maryland's entire bay.

The Assateague Island shoreline is a 37-mile protected National Seashore. Pull your pontoon right up to the bay-side sandbars — some just inches deep. No parking lot. No boardwalk. Just you, the wild horses, and one of the most untouched stretches of coastline on the East Coast.
Six Reasons Your Group Will Love It
Pull your boat right up to the sand on the bay side of Assateague Island National Seashore. The wild horses may walk straight up to you. No other OC rental lets you do this.
Add on our fishing or crabbing package and make it a full-day experience. The bay between Ocean City and Assateague Island is some of the best crabbing on the Eastern Shore.
Bring your own cooler, snacks, and beverages. The driver stays sober — everyone else can enjoy the ride. Ocean City's best floating party is the one you bring yourself.
Dock at Seacrets, Fish Tales, or Fager's Island from the water like a local. Pull right up to Ocean City's most iconic waterfront spots without fighting for parking.
Anchor near Assateague Island and watch the famous horses roam the beach. They've been free since the 1600s. Seeing them from the water is an experience that doesn't exist anywhere else.
Add a tube to your rental and let the kids (or the kids-at-heart) get pulled across the bay. Pure summer fun behind the boat.

Best Activity for Kids in OC

You do. The renter must be 21+ with a valid driver's license. We issue a temporary boater's license at the dock during your orientation. No prior boating experience required — we'll walk you through everything before you leave the dock.
Can we bring our own food and drinks?

Absolutely. Bring your cooler, food, and beverages. The driver must remain completely sober for the entire rental. Passengers 21+ are welcome to enjoy drinks responsibly. Think of it like driving a car — same rules apply on the water.

12 people maximum per boat. Weight limit is 1,900–2,100 lb total (including people, coolers, and gear). This is Maryland maritime law — no exceptions can be made. Count your group and gear before you book.
Where exactly can we take the pontoon?

The entire bay is yours — from the Verrazano Bridge to the Rt. 90 Bridge. Beach on Assateague Island's bay-side sandbars, cruise to Seacrets, Fish Tales, or Fager's Island, drop anchor on a sandbar, or just explore. We give you a full briefing at the dock including route suggestions and must-see spots.
Can we fish and crab from the boat?

Yes! Add our fishing or crabbing package when you book. The packages include gear — you just need a valid Maryland fishing or crabbing license, which are available at local tackle shops around Ocean City MD before you arrive at our dock.

No. We provide a full dock orientation covering navigation, safety rules, operating the boat, and what to do if you have a question out on the water. The bay is calm and the pontoon is easy to handle. First-timers do this every day.
How do we get to Assateague Island by boat?

You beach the pontoon directly on the bay-side of Assateague Island — no parking lot, no trail, no ferry. Just pull up to the shallow sandbars, cut the engine, step off into ankle-deep water, and you're on one of the most beautiful stretches of coastline on the East Coast. We'll show you exactly where to go in your briefing.
Is alcohol allowed on the pontoon boat?

Passengers may consume alcohol. The driver must remain completely sober — this is the same as operating a motor vehicle under Maryland law. We reserve the right to administer a breathalyzer test at any point. Violations end the rental immediately with no refund.

You've Read About It. Now Ride It.
Ocean City MD → Assateague Island National Seashore
The most talked-about jet ski experience on the East Coast. Wild horses. Dolphins. Open ocean. We didn't just name it — we built it.
Where OCA Watersports Earned Its Reputation.

It started in 2010. While every other jet ski rental company in Ocean City Maryland was keeping riders locked in a buoyed-off bay circle, we were thinking about what a real ride could look like. Open water. Real distance. Something worth remembering.

We mapped the bay. We scouted the route from our dock at 12817 Harbor Rd all the way to Assateague Island National Seashore — a 6 mile stretch of water that passed sandbars, million-dollar waterfront homes, open bay, and ended at one of the most protected, untouched shorelines in the entire country.
Every other company in Ocean City that talks about riding to Assateague today? They're following our lead.
"We created the 6 Mile Ride. The unequal watersports rental company — where being equal isn't good enough."
From our dock in downtown Ocean City MD to the wild shoreline of Assateague Island National Seashore. Here's every stop along the way.

You arrive at our dock at 12817 Harbor Rd — right on the bay with direct water access and the biggest riding area in OC. Orientation. Waivers. Briefing on the route south to Assateague. Then you launch.
🌊 Largest riding area in Ocean City MD

Within minutes of launch you're heading south into wide open water — 6 miles of bay stretching along Assateague Island all the way to the Verrazano Bridge. No circles. No buoys. No other company gives you this. Hit the throttle and ride. Million-dollar waterfront homes line the shore. The coastline of Assateague opens up ahead. This is the ride.

As you approach Assateague Island, shallow sandbars rise from the bay floor — some just inches deep. Cut through them. Slow down and drift across them. Pontoon riders beach here. Jet ski riders carve circles. This is where Ocean City's summer crowd completely disappears.

You arrive at Assateague Island National Seashore — a 37-mile barrier island with zero development. No hotels. No condos. Just wild beach, barrier dunes, and the Atlantic. The contrast from Ocean City just 6 miles behind you is staggering. Protected by the National Park Service since 1965.

The famous wild Assateague horses have roamed this island for over 400 years. Legend says they swam ashore from a Spanish shipwreck in the 1500s. Whether true or not, seeing them on the beach — from the water, from your jet ski — is a moment that doesn't exist anywhere else in the world quite like this.
🌅 The best view of Ocean City, Maryland

You turn back toward the OC skyline with the sun dropping behind it and the bay going glassy. This is the most photographed part of the ride — riders slow down here on instinct. You've just done something that most people visiting Ocean City never even know exists. See you back at the dock.

The 6 mile ride was absolutely incredible. No other jet ski place in OC even comes close. You actually get to RIDE, not just go in circles. We saw dolphins and got to cruise by Assateague. Already booked again for next weekend.

We've been coming to Ocean City for 15 years and this was the most unique thing we've ever done here. The route to Assateague, seeing the wild horses from the water — we had no idea this even existed. OCA is in a class by themselves.

Included in Every Jet Ski Rental
The 6 Mile Ride is our signature experience — and it's included in every single jet ski rental. You don't pay extra for the best ride in Ocean City Maryland. You just book and show up.
Ocean City's Most Trusted Watersports Company Since 2010.
We started OCA Watersports in 2010 with a straightforward belief — people deserve better than a 60-minute bay circle. They deserve a real ride. So we gave them one.

We mapped the entire bay between Ocean City and Assateague Island. We found the sandbars, the dolphin feeding grounds, the stretch of beach where the wild horses come down to the waterline. We built a 6 mile route that became the signature experience in Ocean City, Maryland — one that no other company has been able to replicate.

Fifteen years later, the mission hasn't changed. We run the largest riding area in Ocean City. We give riders 70 minutes, not 60. We point out where the horses walk, where the dolphins feed, where the sandbars rise from nowhere at low tide. That ride became the reason thousands of families come back to OCA Watersports year after year.

We are not a babysitter operation. We trust our riders. We give them the whole bay. And we take genuine pride in being the best watersports company in Ocean City, Maryland — not because we say so, but because 640+ Google reviews do.
New name. New home at 12817 Harbor Rd. Bigger riding area. More open water. Better access to Assateague Island and everything the bay has to offer. The ride got an upgrade — and so did we.
